Aspect Model Battery Passport

urn:samm:io.admin-shell.idta.batterypass:1.0.0#BatteryPass
Provides Battery Passport conformant to DIN DKE Spec 99100 and CEN CENELEC EN 18223.

Overview

Properties

Reference standard the DPP instance schema refers to.

SOURCE: EN 18223:2026(en)

dppSchemaVersion
Characteristic

No

Yes

granularity

The granularity of the Product.

There are three values: ‘Model‘, ‘Item‘ and ‘Batch‘.

The term ‘model’ usually refers to a version of a product of which all units share the same technical characteristics relevant for the ecodesign requirements and the same model identifier, the term ‘batch’ usually refers to a subset of a specific model composed of all products produced in a specific manufacturing plant at a specific moment in time and the term ‘item’ usually refers to a single unit of a model.

SOURCE: ESPR p2024/1781, REGULATION (EU) 2024/1781 OF THE EUROPEAN PARLIAMENT AND OF THE COUNCIL of 13 June 2024 establishing a framework for the setting of ecodesign requirements for sustainable products, amending Directive (EU) 2020/1828 and Regulation (EU) 2023/1542 and repealing Directive 2009/125/EC. URI: OJ:L_202401781
Name
granularity
Characteristic
Enumeration
Values
  • Model
  • Batch
  • Item
Type http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#string
Example
Batch
Optional
No
In payload
Yes
Payload key
granularity
Reference
  • https://admin-shell.io/aas/3/1/AssetKind
  • https://admin-shell.io/idta/cds/granularity/1

Entities

AdressInformation

Contains information on how to contact the manufacturer or an authorised service provider, e.g. when a maintenance service is required.
Reference
  • urn:irdi:0173-1#02-AAQ837#005

Properties

role of contact person

Function of a contact person in a process enumeration: 0173-1#07-AAS927#001 (administrativ contact), 0173-1#07-AAS928#001 (commercial contact), 0173-1#07-AAS929#001 (other contact), 0173-1#07-AAS930#001 (hazardous goods contact), 0173-1#07-AAS931#001 (technical contact) Note: the above mentioned ECLASS enumeration should be declared as 'open' for further addition.
Name
roleOfContactPerson
Characteristic
Type http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#string
Example
technical contact
Optional
Yes
In payload
Yes
Payload key
RoleOfContactPerson

CapacityEnergyVoltage

Properties

voltage - NOM

Nominal voltage the battery is rated for.

DIN DKE Spec 99100 chapter reference: 6.7.2.11
Name
nominalVoltage
Characteristic
Measurement
Unit
  • volt
  • Symbol V
  • Code VLT
  • Conversion factor V
Type http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#float
Example
4.3
Optional
No
In payload
Yes
Payload key
NominalVoltage
Reference
  • urn:irdi:0173-1#02-ABL588#001
voltage - MIN

Minimum voltage the battery is rated for.

DIN DKE Spec 99100 chapter reference: 6.7.2.9
Name
minimumVoltage
Characteristic
Measurement
Unit
  • volt
  • Symbol V
  • Code VLT
  • Conversion factor V
Type http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#float
Example
2.04
Optional
No
In payload
Yes
Payload key
MinVoltage
Reference
  • urn:irdi:0173-1#02-ABL587#001
voltage - MAX

Maximum voltage the battery is rated for.

DIN DKE Spec 99100 chapter reference: 6.7.2.10
Name
maximumVoltage
Characteristic
Measurement
Unit
  • volt
  • Symbol V
  • Code VLT
  • Conversion factor V
Type http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#float
Example
6.0
Optional
No
In payload
Yes
Payload key
MaxVoltage
Reference
  • urn:irdi:0173-1#02-ABL589#001
rated capacity

Rated capacity is the total number of ampere-hours (Ah) that can be withdrawn from a fully charged battery under reference conditions (see BattReg Annex IV (1)).

DIN DKE Spec 99100 chapter reference: 6.7.2.2
Name
ratedCapacity
Characteristic
Measurement
Unit
  • ampere hour
  • Symbol A·h
  • Code AMH
  • Conversion factor 3.6 × 10³ C
Type http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#float
Example
210.0
Optional
No
In payload
Yes
Payload key
RatedCapacity
Reference
  • urn:irdi:0173-1#02-ABL869#002
capacity fade

The capacity fade is the Decrease over time and upon usage in the amount of charge that a battery can deliver at the rated voltage, with respect to the original rated capacity (see BattReg Annex IV (2)) as declared by the manufacturer.

DIN DKE Spec 99100 chapter reference: 6.7.2.4
Name
capacityFade
Characteristic
Measurement
Unit
  • percent
  • Symbol %
  • Code P1
  • Conversion factor 1 × 10⁻²
Type http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#float
Example
10.0
Optional
Yes
In payload
Yes
Payload key
CapacityFade
Reference
  • urn:irdi:0173-1#02-ABL828#002
rated energy

The certified usable battery energy is the usable battery energy according to the procedure in the UN GTR No 22 as determined during the certification of the vehicle.

DIN DKE Spec 99100 chapter reference: 6.7.2.5
Name
ratedEnergy
Characteristic
Measurement
Unit
  • kilowatt hour
  • Symbol kW·h
  • Code KWH
  • Conversion factor 3.6 × 10⁶ J
Type http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#float
Example
100.0
Optional
Yes
In payload
Yes
Payload key
CertifiedUsableBatteryEnergy
Reference
  • urn:irdi:0173-1#02-ABL829#002

product carbon footprints

(Set) Balance of greenhouse gas emissions along the entire life cycle of a product in a defined application and in relation to a defined unit of use.
Name
productCarbonFootprints
Characteristic
Set
Has no order
Duplicates not allowed

In payload
Yes

Circularity

Properties

dismantling and removal information

Dismantling and Removal information, including at least:

  • Exploded diagrams of the battery system/pack showing the location of battery cells
  • Disassembly sequences
  • Type and number of fastening techniques to be unlocked
  • Tools required for disassembly
  • Warnings if risk of damaging parts exists
  • Amount of cells used and layout.

BR Annex XIII (2c)

DIN DKE Spec 99100 chapter reference: 6.6.1.2

For more information on the document identified by its document ID please refer to the Handover Documentation of the battery.
Name
dismantlingAndRemovalInformation
Characteristic
Set
Has no order
Duplicates not allowed
Type http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#string
Optional
No
In payload
Yes
Payload key
DismantlingAndRemovalInformation
spare part sources

Contact details of sources for replacement spares. Postal address, including name and brand names, postal code and place, street and number, country, telephone, if any. BR Annex XIII (2b)

DIN DKE Spec 99100 chapter reference: 6.6.1.3
Name
sparePartSources
Characteristic
List
Has a certain order
Duplicates allowed
Type urn:samm:io.admin-shell.idta.batterypass.circularity:1.0.0#SparePartSupplier
Optional
No
In payload
Yes
Payload key
SparePartSources
recycled content

Share of material recovered from waste present in active materials for each battery model per year and per manufacturing plant.

DIN DKE Spec 99100 chapter reference: 6.6.2.3 - 6.6.2.10
Name
recycledContent
Characteristic
List
Has a certain order
Duplicates allowed
Type urn:samm:io.admin-shell.idta.batterypass.circularity:1.0.0#RecycledContent
Optional
No
In payload
Yes
Payload key
RecycledContentInformation
safety measures

Safety measures and instructions should also take past negative and extreme events as well as the separate data attributes 'battery status' and 'battery composition/chemistry' into account.

DIN DKE Spec 99100 chapter reference: 6.6.1.5
Name
safetyMeasures
Characteristic
SingleEntity
Type urn:samm:io.admin-shell.idta.batterypass.circularity:1.0.0#SafetyMeasuresEntity
Optional
No
In payload
Yes
Payload key
SafetyMeasures
end of life information

Producer or producer responsibility organisations shall make information available to distributors and end-users on: the role of end-users in contributing to waste prevention, including by information on good practices and recommendations concerning the use of batteries aiming at extending their use phase and the possibilities of re-use, preparing for re-use, preparing for repurpose, repurposing and remanufacturing.

DIN DKE Spec 99100 chapter reference: 6.6.3.2 - 6.6.3.4
Name
endOfLifeInformation
Characteristic
SingleEntity
Type urn:samm:io.admin-shell.idta.batterypass.circularity:1.0.0#EndOfLifeInformationEntity
Optional
No
In payload
Yes
Payload key
EndOfLifeInformation
renewable content

Share of renewable material content. A renewable material is a material made of natural resources that can be replenished.

DIN DKE Spec 99100 chapter reference: 6.6.2.11
Name
renewableContent
Characteristic
Measurement
Unit
  • percent
  • Symbol %
  • Code P1
  • Conversion factor 1 × 10⁻²
Type http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#float
Optional
No
In payload
Yes
Payload key
RenewableContent
Reference
  • urn:irdi:0173-1#02-ABL867#001

DigitalNameplate

Properties

URI of the product

Unique global identification of the product instance using an universal resource identifier (URI). The battery passport identifier is the unique identifier of a battery passport.

DIN DKE Spec 99100 chapter reference: 6.1.2.1
Name
uriOfTheProduct
Characteristic
Type http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#anyURI
Example
https://dc-qr.com/?m=R123456789
Optional
No
In payload
Yes
Payload key
URIOfTheProduct
Reference
  • urn:irdi:0112/2///61987%23ABN590%23002
  • urn:irdi:0173-1%2302-ABH173%23003
  • urn:samm:io.admin-shell.idta.digital_nameplate:3.0.0#uriOfTheProduct
manufacturer name

Legally valid designation of the natural or judicial person which is directly responsible for the design, production, packaging and labeling of a product in respect to its being brought into circulation.

Information identifying the manufacturer with a name.Information identifying the manufacturer with a name.

DIN DKE Spec 99100 chapter reference: 6.1.2.1
Name
manufacturerName
Characteristic
Set
Has no order
Duplicates not allowed
Type http://www.w3.org/1999/02/22-rdf-syntax-ns#langString
Example
"Muster AG"@de
Optional
No
In payload
Yes
Payload key
ManufacturerName
Reference
  • urn:irdi:0112/2///61987%23ABA565%23009
  • urn:irdi:0173-1%2302-AAO677%23004
  • urn:samm:io.admin-shell.idta.digital_nameplate:3.0.0#manufacturerName
adress of the manufacturer

The manufacturer information postal address, indicating a single contact point. Web address, if available; and web address, if available.

DIN DKE Spec 99100 chapter reference: 6.1.2.3
Name
addressInformation
Characteristic
SingleEntity
Type urn:samm:io.admin-shell.idta.digital_nameplate:3.0.0#AdressInformation
Optional
No
In payload
Yes
Payload key
AddressInformation
Reference
  • https://admin-shell.io/smt-dropin/smt-dropin-use/1/0
  • https://adminshell.io/zvei/nameplate/1/0/ContactInformations/AddressInformation
  • urn:irdi:0112/2///61360_7#AAS002#001
  • urn:irdi:0173-1#02-AAQ837#008/0173-1#01-ADR448#008
  • urn:samm:io.admin-shell.idta.batterypass.digital_nameplate:1.0.0#addressInformation
serial number

Unique combination of numbers and letters used to identify the device once it has been manufactured.

The battery identifier should be serialised, i.e., identifying each battery via a serial number.

DIN DKE Spec 99100 chapter reference: 6.1.2.2
Name
serialNumber
Characteristic
Type http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#string
Example
A12345-X75EN
Optional
No
In payload
Yes
Payload key
SerialNumber
Reference
  • urn:irdi:0112/2///61987%23ABA951%23009
  • urn:irdi:0173-1%2302-AAM556%23004
  • urn:samm:io.admin-shell.idta.digital_nameplate:3.0.0#serialNumber
date of manufacture

Date when an item was manufactured.

The manufacturing date should not only relate to the battery model, but to the battery item. The date code should comply with DINISO8601-1:2020-12 and ISO8601-2:2019.

DIN DKE Spec 99100 chapter reference: 6.1.3.2
Name
dateOfManufacture
Characteristic
Type http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#date
Example
2022-01-01
Optional
No
In payload
Yes
Payload key
DateOfManufacture
Reference
  • urn:irdi:0112/2///61987%23ABB757%23007
  • urn:irdi:0173-1%2302-AAR972%23004
  • urn:samm:io.admin-shell.idta.digital_nameplate:3.0.0#dateOfManufacture
date of putting into service

Where appropriate, the battery passport must include information on the date of putting the battery into service. BR Annex VI Part A (1); Art. 3(33); Art. 38(7); ESPR Art. 2(32)

DIN DKE Spec chapter reference: 6.1.3.3
Name
dateOfPuttingIntoService
Characteristic
Type http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#date
Example
2022-06-01
Optional
Yes
In payload
Yes
Payload key
DateOfPuttingIntoService
unique Identifier of a facility

Unique string of characters for the identification of locations or buildings involved in a product’s value chain or used by actors involved in a product’s value chain.

The manufacturing place should be uniquely identifiable.

DIN DKE Spec 99100 chapter reference: 6.1.3.1
Name
uniqueFacilityIdentifier
Characteristic
Type http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#string
Example
987654321
Optional
No
In payload
Yes
Payload key
UniqueFacilityIdentifier
Reference
  • https://adminshell.io/idta/nameplate/3/0/UniqueFacilityIdentifier
  • urn:irdi:0173-1#02-AAV646#003
  • urn:samm:io.admin-shell.idta.digital_nameplate:3.0.0#uniqueFacilityIdentifier
BatteryStatus

A battery passport must include information on the life cycle status of the battery.

The status of the battery must be defined as 'original' (0173-1#07-ACC020#001), 'repurposed'(0173-1#07-ACC021#001), 're-used'(0173-1#07-ACC022#001), 'remanufactured' (0173-1#07-ACC023#001) or 'waste' (0173-1#07-ACC024#001).

A new battery passport must be issued when a battery was subject to remanufacturing, repurpose or one of the treatment operations preparing for re-use and preparing for repurpose and is placed on the market again.

DIN DKE Spec 99100 chapter reference: 6.1.3.7
Name
batteryStatus
Characteristic
Enumeration
Values
  • original
  • repurposed
  • re-used
  • remanufactured
  • waste
Type http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#string
Example
original
Optional
No
In payload
Yes
Payload key
LifeCycleStage
identifier of the operator

The unique operator identifier should comply with ISO/IEC 15459 1:2014, ISO/IEC 15459 2:2015, ISO/IEC 15459 3:2014, ISO/IEC 15459 4:2014, ISO/IEC 15459 5:2014, ISO/IEC 15459 6:2014, or their equivalent until referenced harmonised standards are listed in the OJEU.

DIN DKE Spec 99100 chapter reference: 6.1.2.3
Name
operatorIdentifier
Characteristic
Type http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#string
Optional
Yes
In payload
Yes
Payload key
OperatorIdentifier
identifier of the manufacturer

A battery passport must include information identifying the manufacturer.

DIN DKE Spec 99100 chapter reference: 6.1.2.4
Name
manufacturerIdentifier
Characteristic
Type http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#string
Optional
No
In payload
Yes
Payload key
ManufacturerIdentifier
Reference
  • urn:irdi:0173-1#02-AAO677#004
markings

Should be used to provide all relevant marking information of the battery passport based on DIN DKE SPEC 99100 such as:

  • Separate collection symbol (6.2.2)
  • Symbols for cadmium and lead (6.2.3)
  • Carbon footprint label (6.2.4)
  • Extinguishing agent (6.2.5)
  • Meaning of labels and symbols (6.2.6)
  • EU declaration of conformity (6.2.7)
  • Results of test reports proving compliance (6.2.8)

Note: CE marking is declared as mandatory according to EU Blue Guide
Name
markings
Characteristic
Set
Has no order
Duplicates not allowed
Reference
  • urn:irdi:0112/2///61360_7%23AAS006%23001
Type urn:samm:io.admin-shell.idta.shared:3.1.0#Marking
Optional
No
In payload
Yes
Payload key
Markings
Reference
  • urn:samm:io.admin-shell.idta.digital_nameplate:3.0.0#markings
EU declaration of conformity

A battery passport must include the EU declaration of conformity.

DIN DKE Spec 99100 chapter reference: 6.2.7

Document identifiers (e.g., depending of different languages) of a document (e.g., PDF) that can be found in the Handover Documentation model.
Name
euDeclarationOfConformity
Characteristic
Set
Has no order
Duplicates not allowed
Type http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#string
Optional
No
In payload
Yes
Payload key
EUDeclarationOfConformity
Reference
  • urn:irdi:0173-1#02-ABA889#003
result of test reports proving compliance

A battery passport must include the test report results that can prove the compliance with the requirements stated in the battery regulation.

DIN DKE Spec 99100 chapter reference: 6.2.8

Document identifiers (e.g., depending of different languages) of a document (e.g., PDF) that can be found in the Handover Documentation model.
Name
resultsOfTestReportsProvingCompliance
Characteristic
Set
Has no order
Duplicates not allowed
Type http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#string
Optional
No
In payload
Yes
Payload key
ResultsOfTestReportsProvingCompliance
Reference
  • urn:irdi:0173-1#02-ABA705#003

Specification of the available time windows.

availableTimeIpCommunicationChannel
Characteristic

Has no order

availableTime
Reference
  • https://admin-shell.io/zvei/nameplate/1/0/ContactInformations/ContactInformation/AvailableTime/

Lifetime

Properties

expected lifetime in calendar years

The battery passport must include information about the expected battery lifetime in calendar years.

The update interval must be upon placement on the market and upon change of the battery status.

DIN DKE Spec 99100 chapter reference: 6.7.6.2
Name
expectedLifetime
Characteristic
Quantifiable
Duration
Unit
  • year
  • Symbol y
  • Code ANN
  • Conversion factor 3.15576 × 10⁷ s
Type http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#unsignedInt
Example
15
Optional
No
In payload
Yes
Payload key
ExpectedLifetimeInCalendarYears
expected number of cycles

Expected battery lifetime expressed in cycles. The exception for non-cycle applications in Article 10 appears sensible, but is not included in the Annex XIII provision. The data attribute is defined by measurement conditions of the cycle-life test such as the C-Rate (see below) and the depth of discharge in the cycle-life test

DIN DKE Spec 99100 chapter reference: 6.7.6.3
Name
expectedNumberOfCycles
Characteristic
Quantifiable
Duration
Unit
  • cycle
  • Code B7
Type http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#unsignedInt
Example
1000
Optional
No
In payload
Yes
Payload key
ExpectedNumberOfCycles
Reference
  • urn:irdi:0173-1#02-ABL830#001
capacity threshold for exhaustion

The percentage of SOCE, above which the battery is still considered operational as EV battery in its current life, as provided by the economic operator.

DIN DKE Spec 99100 chapter reference: 6.7.6.9
Name
capacityThresholdForExhaustion
Characteristic
Measurement
Unit
  • percent
  • Symbol %
  • Code P1
  • Conversion factor 1 × 10⁻²
Type http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#float
Example
23.0
Optional
No
In payload
Yes
Payload key
CapacityThresholdExhaustion
Reference
  • urn:irdi:0173-1#02-ABL838#002
reference test for 'Expected lifetime: Number of charge-discharge cycles'

The battery passport must include information about the expected battery lifetime expressed in cycles, and reference test used.

DIN DKE Spec 99100 chapter reference: 6.7.6.5
Name
cycleLifeReferenceTest
Characteristic
Set
Has no order
Duplicates not allowed
Type http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#string
Optional
No
In payload
Yes
Payload key
CycleLifeReferenceTest
C-rate life cycle test

The C-rate should be provided separately for both the charge and discharge of the battery, if applicable.

The exception for non-cycle applications as mentioned in "Expected lifetime: Number of charge-discharge cycles" should apply to this data attribute as well.

DIN DKE Spec 99100 chapter reference: 6.7.6.6
Name
cRateLifeCycleTest
Characteristic
Quantifiable
Duration
Unit
  • C
  • Symbol C
Type http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#float
Optional
No
In payload
Yes
Payload key
CrateOfRelevantCycleLifeTest

MaterialComposition

Properties

product chemistry

Composition of a product in general terms by specifying the cathode and anode active material as well as electrolyte.

DIN DKE Spec 99100 chapter reference: 6.5.2
Name
batteryChemistry
Characteristic
SingleEntity
Type urn:samm:io.admin-shell.idta.batterypass.material_composition:1.0.1#BatteryChemistry
Optional
No
In payload
Yes
Payload key
BatteryChemistry
battery materials

"Component materials used" (No. 17.a-c): Naming the materials (as a composition of substances) in cathode, anode, electrolyte according to public standards, including specification of the corresponding component (i.e., cathode, anode, or electrolyte). We suggest a reporting threshold of 0.1 % weight by weight.

DIN DKE Spec 99100 chapter reference: 6.5.3-6.5.4
Name
batteryMaterials
Characteristic
List
Has a certain order
Duplicates allowed
Type urn:samm:io.admin-shell.idta.batterypass.material_composition:1.0.1#BatteryMaterial
Optional
No
In payload
Yes
Payload key
BatteryMaterials
hazardous substances

"Hazardous substances" (No 20.a-e): Name (agreed substance nomenclature, e.g. IUPAC or chemical name) all hazardous substance (as 'any substance that poses a threat to human health and the environment'). Suggested above 0.1 % weight by weight within each (sub-)component.

DIN DKE Spec 99100 chapter reference: 6.5.4 - 6.5.6
Name
hazardousSubstances
Characteristic
List
Has a certain order
Duplicates allowed
Type urn:samm:io.admin-shell.idta.batterypass.material_composition:1.0.1#HazardousSubstance
Optional
No
In payload
Yes
Payload key
HazardousSubstances

PowerCapability

Properties

maximum permitted battery power

Maximum permitted power the battery is rated for, includes the data relevant for power limits.
Name
maximumPermittedBatteryPower
Characteristic
Measurement
Unit
  • watt
  • Symbol W
  • Code WTT
  • Conversion factor W
Type http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#float
Example
1.797693E38
Optional
No
In payload
Yes
Payload key
MaximumPermittedBatteryPower
Reference
  • urn:irdi:0173-1#02-ABL843#002
powerFade

Power fade (in %) means the decrease over time and upon usage in the amount of power that a battery can deliver at the rated voltage. (see BattReg Annex IV (4)).

Power fade should be calculated using original and remaining power capability following the formula below: Pfade(x) = (1-P(x)) / PBOL" 100" %" where x is the aging parameter (e.g. storage time, number of cycles, etc.); P(x) is the power capability at aging parameter x; PBOL is the power capability at begin of life.

DIN DKE Spec 99100 chapter reference: 6.7.3.4
Name
powerFade
Characteristic
Measurement
Unit
  • percent
  • Symbol %
  • Code P1
  • Conversion factor 1 × 10⁻²
Type http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#float
Example
23.0
Optional
No
In payload
Yes
Payload key
PowerFade
Reference
  • urn:irdi:0173-1#02-ABL852#002
power capability ratio

The nominal battery power is the suitable approximate value of the power capability used to designate or identify the battery, while the battery energy is determined in reference conditions to be defined.

DIN DKE Spec 99100 chapter reference: 6.7.3.6
Name
powerCapabilityRatio
Characteristic
Measurement
Ratio between nominal allowed battery power (W) and battery energy (Wh).
Unit
  • percent
  • Symbol %
  • Code P1
  • Conversion factor 1 × 10⁻²
Type http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#float
Example
0.611
Optional
No
In payload
Yes
Payload key
RatioNominalBatteryPowerAndBatteryEnergy
Reference
  • urn:irdi:0173-1#02-ABL857#001
original power capability

List of pre-use power capabilities (definition of power capability as given in Battery Regulation).

Power capability shall be measured at reference conditions, which must include measurements at 80% and 20% state of charge for EV and industrial batteries.

DIN DKE Spec 99100 chapter reference: 6.7.3.2
Name
originalPowerCapability
Characteristic
Trait
Reference
Type urn:samm:io.admin-shell.idta.batterypass.technical_data:1.0.1#PowerCapabilityAt
Optional
No
In payload
Yes
Payload key
OriginalPowerCapability
Reference
  • urn:irdi:0173-1#02-ABL853#002
Constraints
  • CardinalityOneToMany
    Min. Length
    1

ProductCondition

Properties

energy throughput

The data attribute should be reported as measured for further potential processing. In addition, the normalisation by capacity may add a further useful value that ensures comparability among battery sizes.

DIN DKE Spec 99100 chapter reference: 6.7.6.7
Name
energyThroughput
Characteristic
SingleEntity
Type urn:samm:io.admin-shell.idta.batterypass.product_condition:1.0.1#EnergyThroughputEntity
Optional
Yes
In payload
Yes
Payload key
EnergyThroughput
capacityThroughput

The data attribute should be reported as measured for further potential processing. In addition, the normalisation by capacity may add a further useful value that ensures comparability among battery sizes.

DIN DKE Spec 99100 chapter reference: 6.7.6.8
Name
capacityThroughput
Characteristic
SingleEntity
Type urn:samm:io.admin-shell.idta.batterypass.product_condition:1.0.1#CapacityThroughputEntity
Optional
Yes
In payload
Yes
Payload key
CapacityThroughput
number of full cycles

Number of (full) charging and discharging cycles.

DIN DKE Spec 99100 chapter reference: 6.7.6.4
Name
numberOfFullCycles
Characteristic
SingleEntity
Type urn:samm:io.admin-shell.idta.batterypass.product_condition:1.0.1#NumberOfFullCyclesEntity
Optional
No
In payload
Yes
Payload key
NumberOfFullCycles
SOCE

Definition based on UNECE GTR 22: The measured or on-board UBE performance at a specific point in its lifetime, expressed as a percentage of the certified usable battery energy.

DIN DKE Spec 99100 chapter reference: 6.7.2.7
Name
stateOfCertifiedEnergy
Characteristic
SingleEntity
Type urn:samm:io.admin-shell.idta.batterypass.product_condition:1.0.1#StateOfCertifiedEnergyEntity
Optional
Yes
In payload
Yes
Payload key
StateOfCertifiedEnergy
Reference
  • urn:irdi:0173-1#02-ABL847#001
remaining energy

Definition from UNECE GTR 22, applicable only to EVs. The energy supplied by the battery from the beginning of the test procedure used for certification until the applicable break-off criterion of the test procedure used for certification is reached.

DIN DKE Spec 99100 chapter reference: 6.7.2.6
Name
remainingEnergy
Characteristic
SingleEntity
Type urn:samm:io.admin-shell.idta.batterypass.product_condition:1.0.1#RemainingEnergyEntity
Optional
Yes
In payload
Yes
Payload key
RemainingEnergy
remaining capacity

The in-use data attribute on capacity, corresponding with the definition of rated capacity.

DIN DKE Spec 99100 chapter reference: 6.7.2.3
Name
remainingCapacity
Characteristic
SingleEntity
Type urn:samm:io.admin-shell.idta.batterypass.product_condition:1.0.1#RemainingCapacityEntity
Optional
Yes
In payload
Yes
Payload key
RemainingCapacity
negative events

The battery passport must contain information and data resulting from its use such as accidents.

DIN DKE Spec 99100 chapter reference: 6.7.8.4
Name
negativeEvents
Characteristic
List
Has a certain order
Duplicates allowed
Type urn:samm:io.admin-shell.idta.batterypass.product_condition:1.0.1#NegativeEvent
Optional
Yes
In payload
Yes
Payload key
NegativeEvents
information on accidents

The battery passport must contain information and data resulting from its use such as accidents.

DIN DKE Spec 99100 chapter reference: 6.7.8.4
Name
informationOnAccidents
Characteristic
Set
Has no order
Duplicates not allowed
Type http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#string
Optional
No
In payload
Yes
Payload key
InformationOnAccidents
Reference
  • urn:irdi:0173-1#02-ABL848#001
temperature information

The battery passport must include periodically recorded information on the operating environmental conditions, including temperature.

DIN DKE Spec 99100 chapter reference: 6.7.7.5 - 8
Name
temperatureInformation
Characteristic
SingleEntity
Type urn:samm:io.admin-shell.idta.batterypass.product_condition:1.0.1#TemperatureConditionsEntity
Optional
No
In payload
Yes
Payload key
TemperatureInformation
remaining power capability

Original power capability (in Watts) and limits, with temperature range when relevant.

  • The amount of energy that a battery is capable to provide over a given period of time under reference conditions.
  • Power capability at 80% and 20% state of charge.

DIN DKE Spec 99100 chapter reference: 6.7.3.3
Name
remainingPowerCapability
Characteristic
SingleEntity
Type urn:samm:io.admin-shell.idta.batterypass.product_condition:1.0.1#RemainingPowerCapabilityEntity
Optional
Yes
In payload
Yes
Payload key
RemainingPowerCapability
Reference
  • urn:irdi:0173-1#02-ABL820#001
evolution of self discharge

Evolution of self-discharge rates is the change of self-discharge over time and usage, as percentage calculated from the initial and current self-discharge rate.

DIN DKE Spec 99100 chapter reference: 6.7.4.8
Name
evolutionOfSelfDischarge
Characteristic
SingleEntity
Type urn:samm:io.admin-shell.idta.batterypass.product_condition:1.0.1#EvolutionOfSelfDischargeEntity
Optional
Yes
In payload
Yes
Payload key
EvolutionOfSelfDischarge
current self discharge rate

The current self-discharge rate is the change of the self-discharge rate in an idle state of the battery in reference conditions (temperature etc.) at aging parameter x, e.g. after a certain amount of storage time or, number of cycles.

DIN DKE Spec 99100 chapter reference: 6.7.4.7
Name
currentSelfDischargingRate
Characteristic
SingleEntity
Type urn:samm:io.admin-shell.idta.batterypass.product_condition:1.0.1#CurrentSelfDischargingRateEntity
Optional
Yes
In payload
Yes
Payload key
CurrentSelfDischargingRate
remaining round trip energy efficiency

The battery passport shall include information, where possible, about the remaining round trip energy efficiency as information on the state of health of the battery

The update frequency of remaining round trip energy efficiency should be aligned with the update frequency of round trip energy efficiency fade and should be provided upon change of the battery status.

DIN DKE Spec 99100 chapter reference: 6.7.4.4
Name
remainingRoundTripEnergyEfficiency
Characteristic
SingleEntity
Type urn:samm:io.admin-shell.idta.batterypass.product_condition:1.0.1#RemainingRoundTripEnergyEfficiencyEntity
Optional
Yes
In payload
Yes
Payload key
RemainingRoundTripEnergyEfficiency
state of charge (SoC)

Available capacity in a battery expressed as a percentage of remaining capacity.

DIN DKE Spec 99100 chapter reference: 6.7.2.8 (changed: available energy or charge in a battery expressed as a percentage of rated capacity as declared by the manufacturer, or, when the battery’s state of health is no longer equal to its initial condition, of the maximum energy or charge that can be stored in the battery at the time of charging)
Name
stateOfCharge
Characteristic
SingleEntity
Type urn:samm:io.admin-shell.idta.batterypass.product_condition:1.0.1#StateOfChargeEntity
Optional
No
In payload
Yes
Payload key
StateOfCharge

Resistance

Properties

initial internal resistance at battery cell level

Internal battery cell and pack resistance / Internal resistance (in Ohm).

Measure of a battery cell’s opposition to current flow at the beginning of its operational life, affecting its performance, efficiency, and heat generation (internal resistance means the absolute value of the quotient of the voltage drop between the terminals by the electric current increase in the battery circuit during a current pulse).
Name
initialInternalResistanceAtBatteryCellLevel
Characteristic
Measurement
Unit
  • ohm
  • Symbol Ω
  • Code OHM
  • Conversion factor Ω
Type http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#float
Example
67.0
Optional
No
In payload
Yes
Payload key
InitialInternalResistanceAtBatteryCellLevel
Reference
  • urn:irdi:0173-1#02-ABL844#002
initial internal resistance at battery pack level

Initial (Pre-Use) internal resistance at battery pack level.

Measure of opposition to current flow in an entire battery pack at the start of its operational life, affecting overall performance, efficiency, and heat generation (internal resistance means the absolute value of the quotient of the voltage drop between the terminals by the electric current increase in the battery circuit during a current pulse).
Name
initialInternalResistanceAtBatteryPackLevel
Characteristic
Measurement
Unit
  • ohm
  • Symbol Ω
  • Code OHM
  • Conversion factor Ω
Type http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#float
Example
23.0
Optional
No
In payload
Yes
Payload key
InitialInternalResistanceAtBatteryPackLevel
Reference
  • urn:irdi:0173-1#02-ABL846#002
initial internal resistance at battery module level

Initial internal resistance at battery module level.

Initial internal resistance means the absolute beginning value of the quotient of the voltage drop between the terminals by the electric current increase in the battery circuit during a current pulse.
Name
initialInternalResistanceAtBatteryModuleLevel
Characteristic
Measurement
Unit
  • ohm
  • Symbol Ω
  • Code OHM
  • Conversion factor Ω
Type http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#float
Example
10.0
Optional
Yes
In payload
Yes
Payload key
InitialInternalResistanceAtBatteryModuleLevel
Reference
  • urn:irdi:0173-1#02-ABL832#001
  • urn:irdi:0173-1#02-ABL832#002
internal resistance increase at battery cell level

Internal resistance increase at battery cell level.
Name
internalResistanceIncreaseAtBatteryCellLevel
Characteristic
Measurement
Unit
  • percent
  • Symbol %
  • Code P1
  • Conversion factor 1 × 10⁻²
Type http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#integer
Example
10
Optional
Yes
In payload
Yes
Payload key
InternalResistanceIncreaseAtBatteryCellLevel
Reference
  • urn:irdi:0173-1#02-ABL831#001
internal resistance increase at battery pack level

'Internal resistance increase at battery pack level' defined by 'increase of internal resistance in % as calculated from current and initial values (calculated from initial and current internal resistance on battery pack level).
Name
internalResistanceIncreaseAtBatteryPackLevel
Characteristic
Measurement
Unit
  • percent
  • Symbol %
  • Code P1
  • Conversion factor 1 × 10⁻²
Type http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#integer
Example
10
Optional
No
In payload
Yes
Payload key
InternalResistanceIncreaseAtBatteryPackLevel
Reference
  • urn:irdi:0173-1#02-ABL831#001
internal resistance increase at battery module level

Internal resistance increase at battery module level level' defined by 'internal resistance increase on battery module level, calculated from initial and current internal resistance on battery module level.
Name
internalResistanceIncreaseAtBatteryModuleLevel
Characteristic
Measurement
Unit
  • percent
  • Symbol %
  • Code P1

10

In payload

  • urn:irdi:0173-1#02-ABL831#001

RoundTripEnergyEfficiency

Properties

initial round trip energy efficiency

Initial round trip energy efficiency means the ratio of the net energy delivered by a battery during a discharge test to the total energy required to restore the initial State of Charge by a standard charge.
Name
initialRoundTripEnergyEfficiency
Characteristic
Measurement
Unit
  • percent
  • Symbol %
  • Code P1
  • Conversion factor 1 × 10⁻²
Type http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#integer
Example
100
Optional
No
In payload
Yes
Payload key
InitialRoundTripEnergyEfficiency
Reference
  • urn:irdi:0173-1#02-ABL833#001
round trip efficiency at 50 percent cycle life

Initial round trip energy efficiency shall be measured at reference conditions that include the specification of 50 % of cycle-life.

Test methods and reference conditions should separately consider non-cycle applications and batteries with very long cycle-life.

DIN DKE Spec 99100 chapter reference: 6.7.4.3
Name
roundTripEfficiencyAt50PercentCycleLife
Characteristic
Measurement
Unit
  • percent
  • Symbol %
  • Code P1
  • Conversion factor 1 × 10⁻²
Type http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#integer
Example
100
Optional
No
In payload
Yes
Payload key
RoundTripEnergyEfficiencyAt50PercentOfCycleLife
Reference
  • urn:irdi:0173-1#02-ABL866#002
energy round trip efficiency fade

Energy round trip efficiency fade is the decrease of round trip energy efficiency as percentage.

DIN DKE Spec 99100 chapter reference: 6.7.4.5
Name
energyRoundTripEfficiencyFade
Characteristic
Measurement
Unit
  • percent
  • Symbol %
  • Code P1
  • Conversion factor 1 × 10⁻²
Type http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#float
Example
10.0
Optional
Yes
In payload
Yes
Payload key
EnergyRoundTripEfficiencyFade
Reference
  • urn:irdi:0173-1#02-ABL827#002
initial self discharging rate

Initial self-discharge in % of capacity per unit of time in defined conditions (temperature range etc) as pre-use metric.

Characteristic

  • percent
  • Symbol %

  • Conversion factor 1 × 10⁻²

Example
2

In payload

InitialSelfDischargingRate

WarrantyInformationEntity

Properties

warranty period

The battery passport must include information about the period for which the commercial warranty applies.

DIN DKE Spec chapter reference: 6.1.3.4

The format shall be provided in ISO 8601 Notation for duration.
Name
warrantyPeriod
Characteristic
Trait
Reference
Type http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#string
Example
P96M
Optional
No
In payload
Yes
Payload key
WarrantyPeriod
Reference
  • urn:irdi:0173-1#02-AAX540#004
Constraints
  • ISO 8601 duration format
    Description
    ISO 8601 duration format.
    Regular expression
    ^P(?!$)((\d+Y)?(\d+M)?(\d+D)?(T(?=\d)(\d+H)?(\d+M)?(\d+S)?)?|T(?=\d)(\d+H)?(\d+M)?(\d+S)?)$

battery location

Battery component that includes the material.

Properties

name
Name
componentName
Characteristic
Type http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#string
Example
Anode
Optional
Yes
In payload
Yes
Payload key
ComponentName
component ID

Identifier of the component.
Name
componentId
Characteristic
Type http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#string
Example
1234-344
Optional
Yes
In payload
Yes
Payload key
ComponentId

capacity throughput

Capacity throughput, last update.

Properties

capacity throughput

Overall sum of the capacity throughput over the battery lifetime (data attribute should be reported as measured for further potential processing. In addition, the normalisation by capacity could add a further useful value that ensures comparability among battery sizes).
Name
capacityThroughputValue
Characteristic
Measurement
Unit
  • ampere hour
  • Symbol A·h
  • Code AMH
  • Conversion factor 3.6 × 10³ C
Type http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#float
Example
214.0
Optional
No
In payload
Yes
Payload key
CapacityThroughputValue
Reference
  • urn:irdi:0173-1#02-ABL849#001

Name
lastUpdate
Characteristic
Type http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#dateTime
Example
2000-01-01T14:23:00
Optional
No
In payload
Yes
Payload key
LastUpdate

  • urn:irdi:0173-1#02-ABG740#003

carbon footprint of a product

Balance of greenhouse gas emissions along the entire life cycle of a product in a defined application and in relation to a defined unit of use.
Reference
  • https://admin-shell.io/idta/CarbonFootprint/ProductCarbonFootprint/1/0

Properties

impact assessment method / calculation method

Standards, methods for determining the greenhouse gas emissions of a product.
Name
pcfCalculationMethods
Characteristic
Set
Has no order
Duplicates not allowed
Type http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#string
Example
ISO 14067
Optional
No
In payload
Yes
Payload key
PcfCalculationMethods
Reference
  • https://admin-shell.io/idta/CarbonFootprint/PcfCalculationMethods/1/0
CO2 eq Climate Change

sum of all greenhouse gas emissions of a product according to the quantification requirements of the standard

DIN DKE Spec 99100 chapter reference: 6.3.2
Name
pcfCo2eq
Characteristic
Measurement
Unit
  • kgCO2e/kWh
  • Symbol kgCO2e/kWh
Type http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#decimal
Example
17.2
Optional
No
In payload
Yes
Payload key
PcfCo2eq
Reference
  • urn:irdi:0173-1#02-ABG855#003
  • urn:samm:io.admin-shell.idta.carbon_footprint:1.0.0#pcfCo2eq
reference value for calculation

Quantity unit of the product to which the PCF information on the CO2 footprint refers.

If a normalized product carbon footprint (PCF) value is provided, the reference value for calculation should be a specific unit such as 'kWh'. If the total PCF value is provided the reference value for calculation should be 'piece'.
Name
referenceImpactUnitForCalculation
Characteristic
Enumeration
Values
  • g
  • kg
  • t
  • ml
  • l
  • cbm
  • qm
  • piece
  • kwH
Reference
  • urn:irdi:0173-1#02-ABG856#003
Type http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#string
Example
piece
Optional
No
In payload
Yes
Payload key
ReferenceImpactUnitForCalculation
Reference
  • urn:irdi:0173-1%2302-ABG856%23003
  • urn:samm:io.admin-shell.idta.carbon_footprint:1.0.0#referenceImpactUnitForCalculation
quantity of measure for calculation

Provides the quantity number of pieces or mass or volume to compute the impact of climate change or product carbon footprint.

If a normalized product carbon footprint (PCF) value is provided, the quantity of measure for calculation should be 1. If the total PCF value is provided the quantity of measure for calculation should be 1.
Name
quantityOfMeasureForCalculation
Characteristic
Quantifiable
Duration
Type http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#double
Example
5.0
Optional
No
In payload
Yes
Payload key
QuantityOfMeasureForCalculation
Reference
  • urn:irdi:0173-1%2302-ABG857%23003
  • urn:samm:io.admin-shell.idta.carbon_footprint:1.0.0#quantityOfMeasureForCalculation
life cycle phases

Life cycle stages of the product according to the quantification requirements of the standard to which the PCF carbon footprint statement refers.

The string value in the value list is based on DIN DKE SPEC 99100: 'A1 - raw material supply (and upstream production)' (6.3.5 required lify cycle mapping), 'A3 - production' (6.3.4 Contribution of main product production/manufacturing lifecycle stage), 'A4 - transport to final destination' (6.3.5 Contribution of distribution lifecycle stage), 'C3 - recycling, waste treatment' (6.3.6 Contribution of end of life and recycling lifecycle stage)

Please note that the model should always contain at least one Product Carbon Footprint entry that reflects the total PCF value (including all life cycle phases) of the battery.
Name
lifeCyclePhases
Characteristic
Set
Has no order
Duplicates not allowed
Type http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#string
Example
C4 - landfill
Optional
No
In payload
Yes
Payload key
LifeCyclePhases
Reference
  • urn:irdi:0173-1%2302-ABG858%23003
  • urn:samm:io.admin-shell.idta.carbon_footprint:1.0.0#lifeCyclePhases
carbon footprint performance class

The battery passport must include the carbon footprint performance class in accordance with the entry into force of the delegated and implementing acts on the carbon footprint performance classes per Article7 of the Battery Regulation.

DIN DKE Spec 99100 chapter reference: 6.3.7
Name
performanceClass
Characteristic
Type http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#string
Example
B+
Optional
No
In payload
Yes
Payload key
PerformanceClass
web link to public carbon footprint study

One or more document identifiers (depending, for example, on the translation) of a specific document, which can be found in the 'HandoverDocumentation' model.

DIN DKE Spec 99100 chapter reference: 6.3.8
Name
webLinkToPublicCarbonFootprintStudy
Characteristic
Set
Has no order
Duplicates not allowed
Type http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#string
Optional
No
In payload
Yes
Payload key
WebLinkToPublicCarbonFootprintStudy
Reference
  • urn:irdi:0173-1#02-ABL848#001

No

Yes

Title
Reference
  • https://api.eclass-cdp.com/0173-1-02-ABG940-003

subtitle

Denotes the focus of the document and has a referential or communicative function, which is to refer to the content, subject matter or theme of the document Constraint: for each language-dependent Title, a Summary and at least one KeyWord shall exist for the given language.
Name
subtitle
Characteristic
Set
Has no order
Duplicates not allowed
Type http://www.w3.org/1999/02/22-rdf-syntax-ns#langString
Optional
Yes
In payload
Yes
Payload key
Subtitle
Reference
  • https://api.eclass-cdp.com/0173-1-02-ABH998-003
  • urn:irdi:0173-1%2302-ABH998%23003
summary

Name
description
Characteristic
Set
Has no order
Duplicates not allowed
Type http://www.w3.org/1999/02/22-rdf-syntax-ns#langString
Optional
Yes
In payload
Yes
Payload key
Description
Reference
  • https://api.eclass-cdp.com/0173-1-02-AAN466-004
  • urn:irdi:0173-1%2302-AAN466%23004
digital files

MIME-Type, file name and file contents

Constraint: at least one PDF/A file type shall be provided. Note: each DigitalFile represents the same content or Document version, but can be provided in different technical formats (PDF, PDFA, html, etc.) or by a link.
Name
digitalFiles
Characteristic
Trait
Reference
Type urn:samm:io.admin-shell.idta.shared:3.1.0#ResourceWithContentType
Optional
No
In payload
Yes
Payload key

Constraints
  • CardinalityOneToMany
    Min. Length
    1

end of life information

Properties

waste prevention

Prevention and management of waste batteries: Point (a) of Article 60(1): Information on the role of end-users in contributing to waste prevention, including by information on good practices and recommendations concerning the use of batteries aiming at extending their use phase and the possibilities of re-use, preparing for re-use, preparing for repurpose, repurposing and remanufacturing
Name
wastePrevention
Characteristic
Set
Has no order
Duplicates not allowed
Type http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#string
Optional
No
In payload
Yes
Payload key
WastePrevention
Reference
  • urn:irdi:0173-1#02-ABL855#001
separate collection

Prevention and management of waste batteries: Point (b) of Article 60(1): Information on the role of end-users in contributing to the separate collection of waste batteries in accordance with their obligations under Article 51 so as to allow their treatment
Name
separateCollection
Characteristic
Set
Has no order
Duplicates not allowed
Type http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#string
Optional
No
In payload
Yes
Payload key
SeparateCollection
Reference
  • urn:idi:0173-1#02-ABL854#001
information on collection

Prevention and management of waste batteries: Point (c) of Article 60(1): information on the separate collection, the take back, the collection points and preparing for re-use, preparing for repurposing, and recycling operations available for waste batteries
Name
informationOnCollection
Characteristic
Set
Has no order
Duplicates not allowed
Type http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#string
Optional
No
In payload
Yes

InformationOnCollection

  • urn:irdi:0173-1#02-ABL856#001

energy throughput

Energy throughput, last update.

Properties

energy throughput

Overall sum of the energy throughput over the battery lifetime (data attribute should be reported as measured for further potential processing. In addition, the normalisation by usable battery energy could add a further useful value that ensures comparability among battery sizes).
Name
energyThroughputValue
Characteristic
Measurement
Unit
  • kilowatt hour
  • Symbol kW·h
  • Code KWH
  • Conversion factor 3.6 × 10⁶ J
Type http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#float
Example
100000.0
Optional
No
In payload
Yes
Payload key
EnergyThroughputValue
Reference
  • urn:irdi:0173-1#02-ABL850#001

lastUpdate
Characteristic
Type http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#dateTime
Example
2000-01-01T14:23:00
Optional
No
In payload
Yes
Payload key
LastUpdate

  • urn:irdi:0173-1#02-ABG740#003

evolution of self discharge

Evolution of self discharge, last update.

Properties

evolution of self discharge value

Total count of times a battery has been fully charged from empty to full capacity and then fully discharged, used as an indicator of the battery's usage and lifespan.

evolutionOfSelfDischargeValue
Characteristic
Measurement
Unit
  • percent
  • Symbol %
  • Code P1
  • Conversion factor 1 × 10⁻²
Type http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#float
Example
1.0
Optional
No
In payload
Yes
Payload key
EvolutionOfSelfDischargeValue

  • urn:irdi:0173-1#02-ABL834#001

file with content type

A file is a data element that represents an address to a file (a locator). The value is a URI that can represent an absolute or relative path.
Reference
  • https://admin-shell.io/aas/3/1/File

Properties

resourceValue

Path and name of the file (with file extension).

The path can be absolute or relative.
Name
resourceValue
Characteristic
Type http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#anyURI
Example
https://example.com/SafetyInstructions.pdf
Optional
Yes
In payload
Yes
Payload key
value
content type

Content type of the content of the file or blob.
Name
contentType
Characteristic
Type http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#string
Example
application/pdf
Optional
Yes
In payload
Yes
Payload key
contentType
Reference
  • https://admin-shell.io/aas/3/1/Blob/contentType
  • https://admin-shell.io/aas/3/1/File/contentType

hazardous substance

Hazardous substances (No. 19-23): Name (agreed substance nomenclature, e.g. IUPAC or chemical name) all hazardous substance (as any substance that poses a threat to human health and the environment'). Suggested above 0.1 % weight by weight within each (sub-)component.

Properties

class

Battery Regulation narrows reporting to substances falling under defined hazard classes and categories of the CLP regulation.
Name
hazardousSubstanceClass
Characteristic
Enumeration
Values
  • AcuteToxicity
  • SkinCorrosionOrIrritation
  • EyeDamageOrIrritation
Type http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#string
Example
SkinCorrosionOrIrritation
Optional
Yes
In payload
Yes
Payload key
HazardousSubstanceClass
name

Clear name of hazardous substance.
Name
hazardousSubstanceName
Characteristic
Type http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#string
Example
Cobalt (Co)
Optional
No
In payload
Yes
Payload key
HazardousSubstanceName
identifier

CAS identifier of hazardous substance.
Name
hazardousSubstanceIdentifier
Characteristic
Trait
Reference
Type http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#string
Example
346417-97-8
Optional
No
In payload
Yes
Payload key
HazardousSubstanceIdentifier
Reference
  • https://www.cas.org/cas-data/cas-registry
Constraints
  • CASNumberConstraint
    Regular expression
    ^\d{2,7}-\d{2}-\d{1}$
concentration

Concentration of hazardous substance.
Name
hazardousSubstanceConcentration
Characteristic
Measurement
Unit
  • percent
  • Symbol %
  • Code P1
  • Conversion factor 1 × 10⁻²
Type http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#double
Example
20.0
Optional
Yes
In payload
Yes
Payload key
HazardousSubstanceConcentration
impact

Impact statements based on, e.g., REACH or GHS for all hazard classes applicable to substances in the battery.
Name
hazardousSubstanceImpact
Characteristic
List
Has a certain order
Duplicates allowed
Type http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#string
Optional
Yes
In payload
Yes
Payload key
HazardousSubstanceImpact
location

Hazardous substances (No. 19-23): Location on a (sub-)component-level of all hazardous substances (as 'any substance that poses a threat to human health and the environment').

Suggested via a unique identifier or nomenclature.
Name
hazardousSubstanceLocation
Characteristic
SingleEntity
Type urn:samm:io.admin-shell.idta.batterypass.material_composition:1.0.1#BatteryComponentEntity
Optional
Yes
In payload
Yes
Payload key
HazardousSubstanceLocation

material

Properties

location

Battery component that relates to the material.
Name
batteryMaterialLocation
Characteristic
SingleEntity
Type urn:samm:io.admin-shell.idta.batterypass.material_composition:1.0.1#BatteryComponentEntity
Optional
No
In payload
Yes
Payload key
BatteryMaterialLocation
CAS Number

Registration number of an international designation standard (CAS = Chemical Abstracts Service) for chemical substances. For each chemical substance registered in the CAS database (also biosequences, alloys, polymers) there is a unique CAS number.
Name
batteryMaterialIdentifier
Characteristic
Trait
Reference
Type http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#string
Example
346417-97-8
Optional
No
In payload
Yes
Payload key
BatteryMaterialIdentifier
Reference
  • https://www.cas.org/cas-data/cas-registry
  • urn:irdi:0173-1#02-ABH034#003
Constraints
  • CASNumberConstraint
    Regular expression
    ^\d{2,7}-\d{2}-\d{1}$
name

Clear name of Material.

Alphanumeric character string which describes the object.
Name
batteryMaterialName
Characteristic
Type http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#string
Example
Lithium
Optional
No
In payload
Yes
Payload key
BatteryMaterialName
Reference
  • urn:irdi:0173-1#02-AAN920#003
weight

Weight of component material in Kilogram.
Name
batteryMaterialMass
Characteristic
Measurement
Unit
  • kilogram
  • Symbol kg
  • Code KGM
  • Conversion factor kg
Type http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#float
Example
52.0
Optional
Yes
In payload
Yes
Payload key
BatteryMaterialMass
is critical raw material

The battery passport must contain information on the critical raw materials present in the battery.

The information on the critical raw materials must also be provided on the battery label. Per Annex VI, Part A(10), critical raw materials must be reported if present in the battery in a concentration of more than 0,1 % weight by weight.
Name
isCriticalRawMaterial
Characteristic
Type http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#boolean
Example
true
Optional
No
In payload
Yes
Payload key
IsCriticalRawMaterial

negative event

Yes

  • percent

  • urn:irdi:0173-1#02-ABL853#001

power capability at reference condition

Power capability measured at a reference condition, for example at 80% or 20% state of charge (SoC). DIN DKE Spec 99100 chapter reference: 6.7.3.2

Properties

state of charge (SoC) in percent

Available capacity in a battery expressed as a percentage of remaining capacity.

Power capability shall be measured at reference conditions, which must include measurements at 80% and 20% state of charge for EV and industrial batteries.
Name
atSoC
Characteristic
Measurement
Unit
  • percent
  • Symbol %
  • Code P1
  • Conversion factor 1 × 10⁻²
Type http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#float
Example
80.0
Optional
No
In payload
Yes
Payload key
atSoC
Reference
  • urn:irdi:0173-1#02-ABL821#001

Type http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#float
Example
500.0
Optional
No
In payload
Yes

recycled content

A battery passport must include recycled content information. The content information must include the percentage share of nickel that is present in active materials and that has been recovered from battery manufacturing waste, for each battery model per year and per manufacturing plant.

Properties

pre consumer share

Recycled material share from pre-consumer waste (manufacturing waste, excluding run-around scrap) of the active material.
Name
preConsumerShare
Characteristic
Trait
Reference
Type http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#float
Optional
No
In payload
Yes
Payload key
PreConsumerShare
Constraints
  • ConsumerShareRange
    >=
    0.0
    ...
    < 100.0
recycled material

Name of recycled material
Name
recycledMaterial
Characteristic
Enumeration
Values
  • Cobalt
  • Nickel
  • Lithium
  • Lead
  • Cobalt
  • Nickel
  • Lithium
  • Lead
Type http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#string
Optional
No
In payload
Yes
Payload key
RecycledMaterial
post consumer share

Recycled material share from post-consumer waste (end-of-life scrap) of the active material.
Name
postConsumerShare
Characteristic
Trait
Reference
Type http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#float
Optional
No
In payload
Yes
Payload key
PostConsumerShare
Constraints
  • ConsumerShareRange
    >=
    0.0
    ...
    < 100.0

Available capacity in a battery expressed as a percentage of remaining capacity.
Name
atSoC
Characteristic
Measurement
Unit
  • percent
  • Symbol %
  • Code P1
  • Conversion factor 1 × 10⁻²
Type http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#float
Example
80.0
Optional
No
In payload
Yes
Payload key
AtSoC

No

remaining round trip energy efficiency

Remaining round trip energy efficiency.

Properties

remaining round trip energy efficiency value

Percentage of energy that can be recovered and used after a full charge and discharge cycle, reflecting the battery's ability to retain and return energy efficiently over time.
Name
remainingRoundTripEnergyEfficiencyValue
Characteristic
Measurement
Unit
  • percent
  • Symbol %
  • Code P1
  • Conversion factor 1 × 10⁻²
Type http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#float
Example
80.0
Optional
No
In payload
Yes
Payload key
RemainingRoundTripEnergyEfficiencyValue

  • urn:irdi:0173-1#02-ABL851#001

lastUpdate
Characteristic

2000-01-01T14:23:00

No
In payload
Yes

  • urn:irdi:0173-1#02-ABG740#003

safety measures

The safety measures should be provided via the instruction manual as URL linking to PDF.

Properties

safety instructions

Necessary safety instructions to handle waste batteries, including in relation to the risks associated with, and the handling of, batteries containing lithium.
Name
safetyInstructions
Characteristic
Set
Has no order
Duplicates not allowed
Type http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#string
Optional
No
In payload
Yes
Payload key
SafetyInstructions
extinguishing agents

Usable extinguishing agents refering to classes of extinguishers (A, B, C, D, K). EUBR: Annex XIII (1a), Annex VI Part A (9)
Name
extinguishingAgents
Characteristic
List
Has a certain order
Duplicates allowed
Type http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#string
Optional
No
In payload
Yes
Payload key
ExtinguishingAgents

single marking information. Note: CE marking is declared as mandatory according to the Blue Guide of the EU-Commission

issue date

Date, at which the specified certificate is issued. Note: format by lexical representation: CCYY-MM-DD. Note: to be specified to the day.

issueDate
Characteristic
Type http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#date
Example
2022-01-01
Optional
Yes
In payload
Yes

spare part supplier

The part numbers for components should be provided together with the postal address, e-mail address and web address of the sources for spare parts. DIN DKE Spec 99100 chapter reference: 6.6.1.3

Properties

company

Name of the company.
Name
company
Characteristic
Set
Has no order
Duplicates not allowed
Type http://www.w3.org/1999/02/22-rdf-syntax-ns#langString
Example
"ABC Company"@en
Optional
No
In payload
Yes
Payload key
NameOfSupplier
Reference
  • urn:irdi:0173-1#02-AAW001#001
address of supplier

Postal address of supplier for spare parts.
Name
addressOfSupplier
Characteristic
SingleEntity
Reference
  • https://schema.org/address
Type urn:samm:io.admin-shell.idta.batterypass.circularity:1.0.0#PostalAddress
Optional
No
In payload
Yes
Payload key
AddressOfSupplier
email

E-mail address and encryption method.
Name
email
Characteristic
SingleEntity
Type urn:samm:io.admin-shell.idta.contact_information:1.0.0#EmailEntity
Optional
No
In payload
Yes
Payload key
EmailAddressOfSupplier
Reference
  • urn:irdi:0173-1#02-AAQ836#005
address of additional link

Web site address where information about the product or contact is given.
Name
addressOfAdditionalLink
Characteristic
Type http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#string
Example
https://example.com/information/product/1224/contact
Optional
No
In payload
Yes
Payload key

  • urn:irdi:0173-1#02-AAQ326#002

Characteristic

No

temperature

Characteristic
Measurement

  • degree Celsius
  • Symbol °C
  • Code CEL
  • Conversion factor 1 × K
Type http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#float
Example
20.0
Optional
Yes
In payload
Yes
Payload key

time extreme high temperature

timeExtremeHighTemp
Characteristic
Measurement
Unit
  • minute [unit of time]
  • Symbol min
  • Code MIN
  • Conversion factor 60 s
Type http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#float
Example
0.0
Optional
Yes
In payload
Yes
